We got paid 11500 + 5*150 = 12250 DOT for the thread and are now living in a period of enlightenment while eating with METAL spoons. Brothers, we have truly reached enlightenment. WE, the Mumbai Warriors will strike yet again and collect generations worth of money !

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Good my brother. The first step to enlightenment is to admit and accept that you're tired. You first need to see your darkness in order for you to bring the light. Those who seek the light have experienced immense darkness. Brother, this is a moment of rejoice. Accept your pain, let it go, move on and forgive yourself or the people who have cause it. But remember brother the world is simply an illusion and by that I mean your own perception of reality. It's all inside you and not in the outside world. A river is not beautiful or magnificent, that's your illusion of it. A river is simply water that is moving, nothing more, nothing less. The illusion feels real because just like when you dream the pain feels real, the fear feels real, the sorrow feels real. Once you wake up from the dream you realize that nothing was real, that it was just an illusion. But it is true that when you were in the dream the pain felt real. So, once we see the illusion for what it is it'll start dissolving and once it dissolves that's then the reason to work inside the world without attachments and to help those who are asleep to wake up.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Once all of those dissolve it'll uncover enlightenment. So, enlightenment isn't an achievement or something you do, it's literally that you stop doing. It's the fact that the doer stops. The watcher and the watched go away. The only thing that remains is the watching.

If you try to tell a person, "Hey bro, there's hope. You're not a loser. I have faith in you. I think you're a good person.", what do they do? They argue with you. They're defended against that. They intellectualize, "There may be hope for other people, but there's no hope for me.". Such a potent intellectual defense mechanism. And what are they defending against? The belief of hope. Because hope hurts. If there's zero chance, there's zero need for effort. See, human beings are really resilient. We can live through genocide and holocaust and all kinds of stuff. We can live through all kinds of terrible things, terrible economic times. We're survivors. But don't give us hope, because if we can hope, we can hurt.

Having hope also exposes you to hurt. And hurt is a normal part of life. If you're hurting that means you're human. It's just. Are you going to hurt consciously and change something or are you going to hurt unconsciously and keep on hurting due to the same thing for the rest of your life?

You maybe haven't "You go this" at the moment. You may feel powerless. You may feel down. And at the same time you'll slowly and gradually regain your power. You'll unburden the weight. Sooner or later you'll feel so light you'll start flying. I really believe that, even though it may at the current moment not feel like it. Even though you might feel like the current moment is never ending trust me, it'll all change and reshape into a healthier future. You got this fren who's reading this.

Your dear Vishnu Kumar wishes you are peaceful day. May Vishnu of the mindchain be with and all around you.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),

item.content:encoded:
What plan is best assuming you have $50k cash?

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-04-29 22:31 UTC

OP

What plan is best assuming you have $50k cash?

A) Wait until there's a major correction and go all in SOXL. Dca out at ATHs.

B) Buy MSTR in June to Dec 2026 (whenever BTC hits 50k). Sell at a 5x-10x.

C) All in BTC in Oct. Then 25% alts. 5% new memes. Sell all in 2-3 years.

D) 30% EMXC, 20% QLD, then later this year 20% OpenAI, 20% Anthropic, 10% SpaceX. Wait 10 years.

62168080
>>62166382
>>62165989
Have a retard tier resume for retail jobs, you're likely over qualified, if you have any college or high tier employment pull if off and fill in with any lies you can get away with. If I knew you irl I could get you jobs or at least interviews but this betrays a larger issue, if you don't know someone personally you're filtered out of a lot of positions. I told myself a two years ago my only plan was to outlast as many others as possible, my only real motivation to continue. I never thought it would get this bad this quickly, the market continues to gaslight everyone, tell them to work harder. The thread a few days ago laughing at homeless Canadians contemplating MAID made me feel sick. Unironically if I had to restart tomorrow I'd buy some used lawn mowers, fix up a shitty used trailer and go back to landscaping for boomers. I know things are bad right now, but it's going to get way worse.

>t. waking up early tomorrow to go into the office and make it worse for literally everyone.

Power bills go up, restructuring continues meaning even more people vying for any job still available and when it turns around the economy restructuring to productive things will take years. A Litecoin bug found today allowed double spend and led to miners having to reorg multiple blocks. PoW is a failed consensus model. Your entire security depends on an assumption that others won't spend more money on attacking the network than miners will defending it, and that those mining will not continue to sell the network out, with Epstein and co. having already permanently neutered it as spendable medium-of-exchange money to transform it into the random evolution of "store of value," controlling the majority of the core devs across a majority of code build and corporatists still controlling a majority of mining. Bitcoin has had a full chain reorg and Nakamoto probabilistic fork choice algo does not give full settlement for this reason. The best incentive is no incentive on networks which are as secure at 1 dollar as they are at 1000 and this is the only model institutions will meaningfully build actual RWA etc. on.

I don't think crypto has a backend issue on the whole anymore, adoption and real use has gone down since a decade ago (I humbly and regrettably spent most of my btc in the mid 2010s on whatever I could buy with it to try and do my small part in getting its notoriety and utility as a currency up). I think the speculation mania of 2017 still lingers like a spectre over the whole ecosystem that disincentivizes spending, because why spend what amounts to a lottery ticket in your account when it could 10x by next week? If some smart contract payment system got executed on a big name chain that devised a payment schedule such as the following:
>see bing bing wahoo on store
>pay in eth (or whatever)
>month passes, get bing bing wahoo
>eth price action explodes
>get smart contract executed arbitrage refund for *part* of the difference in fiat valuation (let's say 75%)
>vendor still profits
>buyer gets good/service, doesn't feel completely fleeced by bad timing
Something like that could make ecommerce and crypto volume explode, and I say that as somebody that thinks every single smart contract platform is cool in theory but absolute ass on execution.
BTW xmr is also my favorite token that I have exactly zero balance for the same reason lmao. Its my long term QQQ/VOO when I'm able to put real money aside for it

item.title: Is 300k at 30 y/o enough to NEET retire? — 30 replies — 5.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is 300k at 30 y/o enough to NEET retire?

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-04-29 16:50 UTC

OP

>24 years old warehouse wagie
>go to work
>go to gym after (hit 2 plate bench milestone a long while ago, lean af, get mired)
>come home, cook, meditate, read, sleep
>minimalist and don't really spend a dime on shit, live with parents rent-free
>no friends, no online friends, no girlfriend, no masturbation, no socializing, no unnecessary spending, no parties, no drugs/alcohol, no fashion, no fastfood/junk, nothing
>have 130k saved up so far, 100k of that invested
So I'm planning to stack up every single penny of my paycheck and I reckon I'd have 300k-500k by 30 years old. I kinda just wanna NEET and chill at my moms basement while my money works for me in the background, then in 10 years time when the 300k becomes a mill I can fully retire with autonomy. I plan on continuing mastering myself without having to adroitly balance a full-time job. I've also transcended most normans in mental and physical acuity so it's quite literally impossible to interact with them in any meaningful capacity; my fulfillment is independent of their proximity. I'm already at the point of my spirituality where indulging in lust has serious palpable physical drawbacks and lethargy (and it's not placebo, I've experimented). So even when I retire there won't be any exorbitant expenses attributed to marriage, kids, travelling, etc.. Also gonna shave my head so no barber. Aside from mastering my ninjutsu when I NEET, as a pass time I'll stick to old vidya / ROM hacks for mastery purposes, so no splurge on ultra normie triple A titles and new consoles/PCs. Can I pull it off?

Early replies (first 3)

Yes with your lifesytle easily. But that lifestyle is better in SEA, we are tropical animals.

62162053
you need to know what your yearly costs are, expected annual returns, then factor in the odds of some negative years. in general you need a lot more money than a few hundred thousand to be able to live off of. with as young as you are, you wouldn’t really want to spend the principal.
